edge_hypothesis: The tail harvester pattern (tight stops + extreme R:R + low win rate + fat-tail capture) is the most important strategic discovery in the system. It has been proven on MACD (PF=1.302-1.442 across 5:1-12:1 R:R) and Supertrend (PF=1.395 at 5:1 R:R). RSI pullback already has an ACCEPT at moderate R:R (PF=1.21, DD=9.2%, 38 trades) using the default configuration: EMA_200 trend filter, EMA_50 slope confirmation, RSI <= 40 pullback threshold. This spec applies the exact tail harvester formula to the proven RSI pullback entry signal WITHOUT changing any entry parameters. The recent rp41w7j3 spec took the WRONG approach: it changed the trend EMA from 200 to 100, producing PF=0.983 with catastrophic DD=264%. That failure confirms the ACCEPT configuration should be preserved. This spec keeps EMA_200, keeps RSI<=40, keeps EMA_50 slope — and ONLY changes the exit structure to tight stop (1.0x ATR) + extreme TP (5.0-8.0x ATR). The RSI pullback entry captures temporary selling pressure in confirmed uptrends. With fat-tailed crypto returns, ~20% of these pullback entries should produce continuation moves of 5-8x ATR. The tight 1.0x ATR stop means false signals are cut immediately — if price doesn't bounce from the pullback within 1 ATR, the trend is weakening and we exit. The 3rd variant tests a deeper pullback (RSI<=35) which selects for more oversold conditions that should produce stronger bounces, at the cost of fewer signals. Invalidation: (1) if both 5:1 and 8:1 produce PF < 1.0, the RSI pullback signal quality is too low for tail harvesting — the entry timing doesn't capture trend continuation moves; (2) if trade count drops below 15, the RSI<=40 threshold combined with EMA_200 on 4h is too restrictive for tail harvesting; (3) if the deep dip variant (RSI<=35) has 0 trades, the threshold is too extreme for 4h.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with proven ACCEPT parameters (EMA_200 trend, EMA_50 slope, RSI<=40) and tail harvester exits: 1.0x ATR stop, 5.0x ATR TP (5:1 R:R). Breakeven win rate: 17%. The RSI pullback ACCEPT (PF=1.21) used moderate R:R — this tests whether the tail harvester pattern that boosted MACD from ~1.0 to 1.442 and Supertrend from 1.264 to 1.395 also works on RSI pullback. If successful, the tail harvester is confirmed as a universal meta-strategy applicable to any decent entry signal.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 confirms uptrend (proven ACCEPT configuration).
EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 40 indicates pullback within uptrend (proven ACCEPT threshold).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms downtrend (proven ACCEPT configuration).
EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms bearish momentum.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 17% win rate to break even after fees.
Tail harvester pattern: tight stop captures false signals immediately, extreme TP captures fat-tail continuation moves.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend filter + EMA_50 slope — proven ACCEPT configuration unchanged
RSI_14 <= 40 pullback threshold — proven ACCEPT threshold, not relaxed
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 5.0x TP — tail harvester exits on proven RSI pullback entry

variants.name: rsi_pullback_tail_harvester_8to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with proven ACCEPT parameters and aggressive tail harvester exits: 1.0x ATR stop, 8.0x ATR TP (8:1 R:R). Breakeven win rate: 11%. This matches the MACD champion's R:R ratio (8:1 → PF=1.442). If the R:R curve shape is universal across templates, 8:1 should outperform 5:1 on RSI pullback just as it did for MACD. The RSI pullback's trend-confirmed dip entry should occasionally catch 8x ATR continuation moves when trends resume strongly after pullbacks.

EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 40 indicates pullback within uptrend (proven ACCEPT threshold).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms downtrend (proven ACCEPT configuration).
EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms bearish momentum.

exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11% win rate to break even after fees.
Tests whether MACD R:R curve peak at 8:1 replicates on RSI pullback.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend filter + EMA_50 slope — proven ACCEPT configuration unchanged
RSI_14 <= 40 pullback threshold — matches proven ACCEPT
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 8.0x TP — tests MACD champion R:R (8:1) on RSI pullback

variants.name: rsi_pullback_deep_dip_tail_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with TIGHTER entry quality (RSI<=35 instead of 40) and tail harvester exits: 1.0x ATR stop, 5.0x ATR TP (5:1 R:R). The hypothesis: deeper pullbacks (RSI<=35) select for more oversold conditions that produce stronger mean-reversion bounces. Combined with tail harvester exits, these rare, high-quality dip entries should produce larger continuation moves. Advisory notes RSI<=30 produces 0 trades on 1h — but RSI<=35 on 4h is untested and should generate signals. Comparison with the 5:1 variant (RSI<=40) isolates whether entry quality matters more than signal quantity for the tail harvester pattern.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 confirms uptrend (proven ACCEPT configuration).
EMA_50 slope rising (current > previous bar) confirms medium-term momentum.
RSI_14 <= 35 indicates DEEP pullback within uptrend (tighter than ACCEPT threshold).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms downtrend (proven ACCEPT configuration).
EMA_50 slope falling (current < previous bar) confirms bearish momentum.
RSI_14 >= 65 indicates deep pullback within downtrend.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 17% win rate to break even after fees.
Deep dip variant: tests whether tighter entry quality (RSI<=35) improves tail harvester performance.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend filter + EMA_50 slope — proven ACCEPT configuration unchanged
RSI_14 <= 35 DEEP pullback threshold — tighter than ACCEPT to select higher-quality entries
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 5.0x TP — tail harvester on deep dip entries
